definitions: The apparent displacement or the difference in apparent direction of an object as seen from two different points not on a straight line with the object.
The effect of parallax, especially the apparent change in the position of a celestial body due to the change in the observer's position.
An error in reading an instrument due to the observer's eye being at an angle to the indicator.
examples: Astronomers use parallax to measure distances to stars.
The parallax error made the reading inaccurate.
Binoculars often have adjustments to correct for parallax.
